data.title: Microservices
data.description: Google uses a microservices architecture to ensure scalability and maintainability.
data.technical_spec: Hundreds of independent services communicate via APIs. Built with containers and orchestration tools like Kubernetes.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Distributes network traffic efficiently across multiple servers.
data.technical_spec: Uses global load balancers to handle user requests and ensure availability.

data.title: Data Centers
data.description: Highly secure and optimized for performance, Google's data centers ensure global access.
data.technical_spec: Custom-built infrastructure with proprietary hardware and open-source software.

data.title: Redundancy
data.description: Ensures data is stored in multiple locations to prevent loss and downtime.
data.technical_spec: Geographically dispersed data centers with failover mechanisms.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Speeds up content delivery by distributing it closer to users.
data.technical_spec: Edge network points around the world cache content to minimize latency.
